title: Dissociation of Nuclear Suspensions
author: Emi Sei
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.t3aeqie

steps.title: Cut fresh or frozen tissue (about...
steps.instruction: Cut fresh or frozen tissue (about 1x1x1cm) and mince it with a scalpel in a 10-cm Petri dish with 2 mL, NST-DAPI buffer for 00:10:00 (10-15 min) until tissue chunks are no longer visible.

steps.title: Filter the mixture through a 40 ul flowmi into 1
steps.instruction: Filter the mixture through a 40 ul flowmi into 1.5 ml DNA LoBind tubes. Place the tube on ice.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend with...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend with 1 mL, cold Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer . (1/3)

steps.title: Wash again with 1 mL, cold...
steps.instruction: Wash again with 1 mL, cold Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer . (2/3)

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in cold Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in cold Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer. Count nuclei (Make a 1:1 solution of Hoechst and PI, pipette 2 µL of this mix to 10 µL, filtered nuclei and count in the Countess II FL).

steps.title: Proceed immediately with 10X genomics RNA...
steps.instruction: Proceed immediately with 10X genomics RNA experiments or single cell DNA sequencing experiments.

steps.title: Wash again with 1 mL, cold...
steps.instruction: Wash again with 1 mL, cold Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer . (3/3)
